opp.name: Tyson Foods_ANA
opp.next_step: 25% confidence 26th May- Followed up to Carlos for further steps 27th April- Carlos has been in contact asking to re-look at S2P and now ANA is more pressing 22nd April Carlos Rodriges (CPO) is interested still, since Thai RT- follow up. They’re open to exploring a demo focused on indirect automation (PR to PO + negotiation)
opp.ais_why:
• Per the 22 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Carlos Rodriges (CPO) is interested still, since Thai RT- follow up. They're open to exploring a demo focused on indirect automation (PR to PO + negotiation).' Per 27 Apr 2026: 'Carlos has been in contact asking to re-look at S2P and now ANA is more pressing.' CPO Carlos Rodriges is the named champion who has flagged ANA (autonomous negotiation) as 'more pressing' ‚Äî a direct buyer-side AI-product prioritisation.
• Product scope is eProcurement; Merlin; ANA ‚Äî both Merlin and ANA in scope, with the opp name explicitly 'Tyson Foods_ANA'. The Thai RT event triggered the engagement and Carlos is asking to re-look at the full S2P with ANA now becoming a leading driver. Indirect automation (PR-to-PO + negotiation) is the named demo focus.
• Confidence is Medium because the NextStep is short (276 chars) but every sentence is AI-substantive: named CPO + named AI product + buyer-stated priority shift toward ANA. BD_Engagement is null in SFDC (data lag likely). No demo completed yet. Score 7 reflects buyer-led AI demand with named champion but pending demo execution; would move to 8-9 with a successful POC or formal evaluation criteria.

items.quote: No EB identified; Carlos is Decision Maker but no evidence he controls $150k budget or final sign-off
Single contact (Carlos); no engagement with CFO, Deputy Procurement Director, or other stakeholders on account roster
No financial impact, savings target, or ROI case articulated by buyer
No visibility into evaluation steps, approval chain, or buyer's internal timeline from demo to contract
No discussion of MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or procurement channel (partner-led or direct)
Coupa listed in Competitors__c but no recent buyer discussion of their standing, pricing, or evaluation status

sources: Next_Step__c (27 April 2026: ANA is more pressing)
Avoma discovery call 01 Dec 2025 (no business discussion captured)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business value case has been articulated. The Next_Step log references that Carlos asked to 're-look at S2P and now ANA is more pressing' on 27 April 2026, signaling that spend analytics (ANA) has become a priority pain point, but no specific financial impact, headcount savings, cycle-time reduction, or ROI target has been stated by the buyer. The discovery call on 01 December 2025 captured only greetings and technical setup, with no substantive business discussion recorded. Without a quantified value case the deal lacks a compelling business rationale to drive urgency or executive sponsorship. The immediate move is to schedule a discovery or scoping session with Carlos and his finance/procurement leadership to quantify the pain: current manual effort, error rates, spend leakage, or compliance risk that ANA and indirect procurement automation would address.

sources: Next_Step__c (27 April 2026 interest signal, 26 May follow-up unanswered)
Avoma and tasks (no multi-threading or advocacy evidence)
champion.narrative: Carlos Rodimar Rodrigues (International Procurement Director - SEA & EU, Decision Maker contact role) is the sole engaged stakeholder and the only candidate for champion, but his advocacy is unproven and his engagement is cooling. He initiated renewed contact on 27 April 2026 asking to 're-look at S2P and now ANA is more pressing,' which signals active interest at that moment (source: Next_Step__c). However, the rep's 26 May follow-up to Carlos for 'further steps' has gone unanswered for 31 days as of today (26 June), indicating that his engagement has stalled. There is no evidence that Carlos has introduced us to other stakeholders, shared internal documents, defended Zycus against Coupa, or driven the agenda forward. We do not know if he has access to the Economic Buyer or budget authority, and we have not multi-threaded beyond him. A single-threaded deal with a cooling contact is high-risk. The champion is weak and at risk of ghosting. The immediate move is to re-engage Carlos with a value-add touch (a relevant proof point, ROI calculator, or peer reference) and simultaneously ask for introductions to his finance and procurement leadership to build redundancy and executive sponsorship.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the named competitor per the Salesforce Competitors__c field, but we have no recent intelligence on their positioning, pricing, or the buyer's sentiment toward them. The Next_Step log and the single discovery call provide no mention of Coupa or any other rival, so the competitive read is stale and unconfirmed in recent buyer conversations. We do not know if Coupa is actively in the evaluation, if Tyson is an existing Coupa customer, or if this is a greenfield opportunity. On a deal where ANA and indirect automation are the stated priorities, Coupa's broad platform and brand strength make them a credible threat, but without recent buyer feedback we cannot assess their true standing. The wedge to win is Merlin's negotiation automation and Zycus's depth in indirect procurement workflows (PR to PO), but we have not yet positioned this differentiation in front of Carlos or tested it against Coupa's offering. The recommended move is to run a discovery or demo session that explicitly surfaces how the buyer is addressing these needs today, what they know about Coupa's capabilities, and where they see gaps—then position Merlin and our indirect automation strength as the superior fit.

sources: Next_Step__c (27 April 2026: ANA is more pressing)
Next_Step__c (22 April 2026: demo on indirect automation PR to PO + negotiation)
OpportunityContactRole (Carlos as pain owner, Decision Maker)
identify_pain.narrative: Carlos has articulated two operational pain points but without quantification. First, on 27 April 2026 he stated that 'ANA is more pressing,' signaling an urgent need for spend analytics and visibility (source: Next_Step__c). Second, the 22 April note records that they are open to a demo on 'indirect automation (PR to PO + negotiation),' implying pain around manual requisition-to-PO workflows and supplier negotiation inefficiency. However, we do not know the financial or operational impact: how many indirect purchase requisitions they process monthly, current cycle time, error rate, compliance failures, or savings leakage. The pain owner is Carlos (International Procurement Director SEA & EU, Decision Maker role), but we have not confirmed whether he personally owns the budget to address it or if the pain is shared with finance (CFO Albert Yeoh) or a regional operations leader. The next step is to run a quantified pain discovery session with Carlos and ideally a finance stakeholder to turn qualitative urgency into a measurable business case.

sources: No evidence in Next_Step__c, tasks, or Avoma calls
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec requirements, and procurement channel are entirely unknown. There is no mention in Next_Step, tasks, or the discovery call of MSA/SOW needs, whether Tyson has a preferred vendor onboarding process, or if this deal will route through an SI or reseller partner (the ground truth note flags this as possibly partner-led, but no partner is named in the evidence). On an APAC enterprise account, InfoSec and data residency reviews are common gates, but none have been discussed. Without visibility into the paper process we cannot forecast the true time to close. The move is to surface the contracting and compliance path early: ask Carlos whether Tyson's legal/procurement/InfoSec teams will need to review our agreements, what their standard cycle time is, and if a partner or regional entity will be the contracting party.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Carlos: Decision Maker, no EB role assigned)
Account contacts (Albert Yeoh CFO, Tachaya Punsuwan Deputy Director — not engaged)
Next_Step__c and Avoma (no EB or budget authority discussion)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. Carlos Rodimar Rodrigues holds the Decision Maker contact role (International Procurement Director - SEA & EU) and is the sole buyer-side contact on this opportunity, but there is no evidence across calls, tasks, or Next_Step logs that he controls the commercial budget or has final sign-off authority for a $150k investment. The account roster lists Albert Yeoh (CFO) and Tachaya Punsuwan (Deputy Procurement Director) as potential budget holders, but neither has been engaged on this deal. On a partner-led APAC deal it is common for the EB to surface late, but at 7 months in Qualified stage with no EB mapped and no pricing or commercials discussion logged, this is a critical gap. The ranked move is to ask Carlos directly who will approve the budget and sign the contract, then secure an introduction to that executive before any demo or proposal is delivered.

sources: Avoma discovery call 01 Dec 2025 (no process discussion)
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. Only one stakeholder (Carlos) has been engaged, and there is no record of who else will participate in the evaluation, how vendor selection will be run (RFI, RFP, bake-off, or sole-source), or what approval steps are required before contract signature. The Next_Step log and the single discovery call provide no insight into whether this is a formal procurement-led process or an informal pilot exploration. On a $150k APAC deal with a CPO involved, a multi-stakeholder committee and finance/legal review are likely, but we have not mapped them. The absence of a documented process after 7 months in Qualified is a red flag that the buyer may be in early exploration mode with no formal mandate to purchase. The immediate action is to ask Carlos to walk us through the steps from demo to contract signature, who will be involved at each gate, and what timeline he is working to.

sources: Next_Step__c (22 April 2026: demo on indirect automation PR to PO + negotiation)
Next_Step__c (27 April 2026: ANA is more pressing)
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer has signaled interest in a demo focused on indirect automation, specifically PR-to-PO workflow and negotiation capabilities, per the 22 April 2026 Next_Step note. This suggests their decision criteria center on indirect procurement efficiency and automation depth. However, no formal RFP, scoring rubric, or evaluation framework has been shared. The discovery call on 01 Dec 2025 did not capture use-case requirements or success criteria. We do not know whether they are evaluating ease of integration with their ERP, user adoption/change management, Merlin AI differentiation, or ROI payback period. The partial signal is that ANA (spend analytics) has become 'more pressing' as of 27 April, indicating that analytics and visibility are rising in priority. The next move is to run a structured discovery or BRD alignment session to document their top 3-5 decision criteria, weightings, and any must-have vs nice-to-have features before delivering the demo.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling. Carlos expressed interest in April (ANA pressing, open to demo) but has not replied to 26 May follow-up (31 days ago). Single-threaded, no EB, no champion advocacy, no progression from Qualified in 7 months. Engagement is thinning.

north_star_verdict.math: 157 days to close (2026-11-30). Created 2025-12-01, ~7 months in Qualified stage with no progression to Formal Evaluation. Last activity 2026-05-26 (31 days ago). Next_Step indicates 26 May follow-up to Carlos with no buyer response logged. Deal is cooling after April interest signal.

26 May 2026: Rep followed up to Carlos for further steps (source: Next_Step__c)

Single contact thread (Carlos only), no multi-threading, no EB identified, no champion developed
Pipeline forecast not defensible at Qualified stage with 157 days to close, no formal evaluation activity, and cooling engagement
north_star_verdict.headline: CPO Carlos expressed April interest in S2P/ANA but has gone silent for 31 days since May 26 follow-up. Single-threaded, no demo scheduled, 7 months stuck in Qualified with zero progression—deal is cooling without buyer engagement or multi-threading.

best_practice_check.summary: Single-threaded deal with cooling engagement; must multi-thread to CFO/Deputy Director and map EB before any demo. Coupa is named competitor but differentiation story (Merlin negotiation automation) not yet positioned. No champion evidence or quantified value case to drive urgency.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the only named competitor (source: Salesforce Competitors__c field), but competitive intelligence is stale with no recent buyer discussion of alternatives captured since deal creation in December 2025. Threat level is medium by brand strength and platform breadth alone, but actual engagement status, buyer sentiment, and Coupa's pricing/positioning are unknown. If Tyson is evaluating Coupa actively, their broad suite and market presence make them a credible peer threat. Zycus wins by leading with Merlin negotiation automation and deep indirect procurement workflow fit (PR to PO), which we have not yet positioned. The immediate move is to run discovery that surfaces the buyer's current approach, any Coupa exposure, and their evaluation criteria, then demo Merlin and indirect automation as the differentiated solution.

Make Merlin the centerpiece of the demo and tie it to the buyer's stated ANA urgency.
